args.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load all businesses with WiFi
data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:52']
if isinstance(data, str):
    with open(data, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    businesses = data

print('Processing %d businesses with WiFi attribute' % len(businesses))

# Find all WiFi businesses and extract their states
wifi_businesses = []
state_counts = Counter()

for biz in businesses:
    # Parse WiFi value
    wifi_str = str(biz['attributes']['WiFi'])
    wifi_val = None
    
    if wifi_str.startswith("u'") or wifi_str.startswith('u"'):
        wifi_val = wifi_str[2:-1]
    else:
        wifi_val = wifi_str.strip("'\"")
    
    # Check if it's actually WiFi (not 'no')
    if wifi_val and wifi_val.lower() in ['free', 'yes', 'paid', 'true']:
        # Extract state from description
        desc = biz.get('description', '')
        if desc and desc != 'None':
            match = re.search(r'([A-Z]{2})', desc)
            if match:
                state = match.group(1)
                biz_id = biz['business_id']
                ref_id = biz_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                
                wifi_businesses.append({
                    'ref': ref_id,
                    'state': state,
                    'name': biz['name']
                })
                state_counts[state] += 1

print('Found %d WiFi businesses across %d states' % (len(wifi_businesses), len(state_counts)))

# Get top state
top_states = state_counts.most_common()
if top_states:
    top_state = top_states[0][0]
    top_count = top_states[0][1]
    print('Top state is %s with %d WiFi businesses' % (top_state, top_count))
    
    # Get all business_refs for this state
    top_state_refs = [b['ref'] for b in wifi_businesses if b['state'] == top_state]
    print('Business refs for %s: %d' % (top_state, len(top_state_refs)))
else:
    top_state = None
    top_state_refs = []

# Save for rating lookup
with open('/tmp/wifi_businesses.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(wifi_businesses, f)

with open('/tmp/top_state_refs.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(top_state_refs, f)

result = {
    'wifi_total': len(wifi_businesses),
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_state_count': len(top_state_refs),
    'top_state_refs': top_state_refs[:3]  # Sample
}
print('__RESULT__:' + json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

            wifi_val = None
            
            if wifi_str.startswith("u'") or wifi_str.startswith('u"'):
                wifi_val = wifi_str[2:-1]

                desc = biz.get('description', '')

                    if match:
                        state = match.group(1)
                        biz_id = biz['business_id']
                        ref_id = biz_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')

                        })
                        state_counts[state] += 1

print('Found ' + str(len(wifi_businesses)) + ' WiFi businesses across ' + str(len(state_counts)) + ' states')

if top_states:

val_args.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load all businesses with WiFi attributes
biz_data = locals()["var_functions.query_db:52"]
if isinstance(biz_data, str):
    with open(biz_data, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    businesses = biz_data

print('Processing ' + str(len(businesses)) + ' businesses with WiFi attribute')

# Find all WiFi businesses and count by state
wifi_businesses = []
state_counts = Counter()

for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi is not None:
            wifi_str = str(wifi)
            wifi_val = None
            
            if wifi_str.startswith("u'") or wifi_str.startswith('u"'):
                wifi_val = wifi_str[2:-1]
            else:
                wifi_val = wifi_str.strip("'\"")
            
            if wifi_val and wifi_val.lower() in ['free', 'yes', 'paid', 'true']:
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                if desc and desc != 'None':
                    match = re.search(r'([A-Z]{2})', desc)
                    if match:
                        state = match.group(1)
                        biz_id = biz['business_id']
                        ref_id = biz_id.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                        
                        wifi_businesses.append({
                            'ref': ref_id,
                            'state': state
                        })
                        state_counts[state] += 1

print('Found ' + str(len(wifi_businesses)) + ' WiFi businesses across ' + str(len(state_counts)) + ' states')

# Get top state
top_states = state_counts.most_common()
if top_states:
    top_state = top_states[0][0]
    top_count = top_states[0][1]
    print('Top state: ' + top_state + ' with ' + str(top_count) + ' WiFi businesses')
    
    top_state_refs = [b['ref'] for b in wifi_businesses if b['state'] == top_state]
    print('Business refs for ' + top_state + ': ' + str(len(top_state_refs)))
else:
    top_state = None
    top_state_refs = []

# Save data for rating lookup
with open('/tmp/wifi_businesses.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(wifi_businesses, f)

with open('/tmp/top_state_refs.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(top_state_refs, f)

result = {
    'wifi_total': len(wifi_businesses),
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_count': len(top_state_refs)
}
print('__RESULT__:' + json.dumps(result))

args.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load business data from the latest query
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:88']
if isinstance(businesses, str):
    with open(businesses, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)

print('Processing', len(businesses), 'businesses')

# Find all WiFi businesses with states
wifi_businesses = []
state_counts = Counter()

for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi is not None:
            # Parse WiFi value safely
            wifi_str = str(wifi)
            if wifi_str.startswith("u'") and wifi_str.endswith("'"):
                wifi_val = wifi_str[2:-1]
            else:
                wifi_val = wifi_str.strip("'\"")
            
            # Check if it's a WiFi business (not 'no')
            if wifi_val and wifi_val.lower() in ['free', 'yes', 'paid', 'true']:
                # Get state from description
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                if desc and desc != 'None' and isinstance(desc, str):
                    m = re.search(r'\b([A-Z]{2})\b', desc)
                    if m:
                        state = m.group(1)
                        ref_id = biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                        wifi_businesses.append({'ref': ref_id, 'state': state, 'name': biz['name']})
                        state_counts[state] += 1

print('Found', len(wifi_businesses), 'WiFi businesses')
print('Top 10 states:', state_counts.most_common(10))

# Get top state
top_state_info = state_counts.most_common(1)[0] if state_counts else None
if top_state_info:
    top_state, top_count = top_state_info
    print('Top state:', top_state, 'with', top_count, 'businesses')
    # Get refs for top state
    top_refs = [b['ref'] for b in wifi_businesses if b['state'] == top_state]
else:
    top_state = None
    top_refs = []

# Save results
with open('/tmp/wifi_businesses.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(wifi_businesses, f)

if top_refs:
    with open('/tmp/top_state_refs.json', 'w') as f:
        json.dump(top_refs, f)

result = {
    'wifi_total': len(wifi_businesses),
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_count': len(top_refs)
}
print('__RESULT__:' + json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json, re, os
from collections import Counter

# Get business file path
biz_file = var_functions.query_db:88
if isinstance(biz_file, str):
    with open(biz_file, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    businesses = biz_file

# Process businesses
wifi_list = []
state_counts = Counter()

for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi is not None:
            wstr = str(wifi)
            wval = wstr[2:-1] if wstr.startswith("u'") else wstr.strip("'\"")
            if wval and wval.lower() in ['free', 'yes', 'paid']:
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                if desc and desc != 'None':
                    m = re.search(r'([A-Z]{2})', desc)
                    if m:
                        state = m.group(1)
                        ref = biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                        wifi_list.append({'ref': ref, 'state': state})
                        state_counts[state] += 1

# Get top state
top = state_counts.most_common(1)[0] if state_counts else None
if top:
    top_state = top[0]
    top_refs = [b['ref'] for b in wifi_list if b['state'] == top_state]
else:
    top_state = None
    top_refs = []

# Save
with open('/tmp/wifi_list.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(wifi_list, f)
with open('/tmp/top_refs.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(top_refs, f)

result = {'wifi_total': len(wifi_list), 'top_state': top_state, 'top_count': len(top_refs)}
print('__RESULT__:' + json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:92.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

args.code:
import json, re, os
from collections import Counter

# Load business data
biz_path = var_functions.query_db:88
if isinstance(biz_path, str):
    with open(biz_path, 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
else:
    businesses = biz_path

# Find WiFi businesses
wifi_list = []
state_counts = Counter()

for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi is not None:
            wstr = str(wifi)
            if wstr.startswith("u'"):
                wval = wstr[2:-1]
            else:
                wval = wstr.strip("'")
            if wval and wval.lower() in ['free', 'yes', 'paid']:
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                if desc and desc != 'None':
                    m = re.search(r'([A-Z]{2})', desc)
                    if m:
                        state = m.group(1)
                        ref = biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                        wifi_list.append({'ref': ref, 'state': state})
                        state_counts[state] += 1

# Get top state
top_state = None
top_refs = []
if state_counts:
    top = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    top_state = top[0]
    top_refs = [b['ref'] for b in wifi_list if b['state'] == top_state]

# Save data
with open('/tmp/wifi_list.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(wifi_list, f)
with open('/tmp/top_refs.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(top_refs, f)

result = {'wifi_total': len(wifi_list), 'top_state': top_state, 'top_count': len(top_refs)}
print('__RESULT__:' + json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Load business data
biz_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:88']
businesses = biz_data if isinstance(biz_data, list) else json.load(open(biz_data))

wifi_list = []
state_counts = Counter()

for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi is not None:
            wstr = str(wifi)
            wval = wstr[2:-1] if wstr.startswith("u'") else wstr.strip("'\"")
            if wval and wval.lower() in ['free', 'yes', 'paid']:
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                if desc and desc != 'None':
                    m = re.search(r'([A-Z]{2})', desc)
                    if m:
                        state = m.group(1)
                        ref = biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                        wifi_list.append({'ref': ref, 'state': state})
                        state_counts[state] += 1

# Get top state
top_state = None
top_refs = []
if state_counts:
    top = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    top_state = top[0]
    top_refs = [b['ref'] for b in wifi_list if b['state'] == top_state]

# Save results
with open('/tmp/wifi_list.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(wifi_list, f)
with open('/tmp/top_refs.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(top_refs, f)

result = {
    'wifi_total': len(wifi_list),
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_count': len(top_refs)
}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

from collections import Counter

# Load business data
biz_data = locals()['var_functions.query_db:88']
businesses = biz_data if isinstance(biz_data, list) else json.load(open(biz_data))

wifi_list = []
state_counts = Counter()

for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if attrs and isinstance(attrs, dict):
        wifi = attrs.get('WiFi')
        if wifi is not None:
            wstr = str(wifi)
            wval = wstr[2:-1] if wstr.startswith("u'") else wstr.strip("'\"")
            if wval and wval.lower() in ['free', 'yes', 'paid']:
                desc = biz.get('description', '')
                if desc and desc != 'None':
                    m = re.search(r'([A-Z]{2})', desc)
                    if m:
                        state = m.group(1)
                        ref = biz['business_id'].replace('businessid_', 'businessref_')
                        wifi_list.append({'ref': ref, 'state': state})
                        state_counts[state] += 1

# Get top state
top_state = None
top_refs = []
if state_counts:
    top = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    top_state = top[0]
    top_refs = [b['ref'] for b in wifi_list if b['state'] == top_state]

# Save results
with open('/tmp/wifi_list.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(wifi_list, f)
with open('/tmp/top_refs.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(top_refs, f)

result = {
    'wifi_total': len(wifi_list),
    'top_state': top_state,
    'top_count': len(top_refs)
}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:12.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened
